macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls 2% for Fifth Straight Weekly Loss Amid Mideast Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 2% last week, marking five consecutive weekly losses for the first time since 2022, and is on pace for its worst month in a year due to Middle East conflict jitters. U.S. 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43% while Brent crude surged to $112 per barrel, raising concerns over central banks' ability to combat rising inflation.

items.title: BlackRock Highlights Energy Security and AI Power Demand Thematic Opportunities
items.summary: Middle East conflict and AI-driven power demand are boosting investments in energy infrastructure, supply chain resilience, commodities, and defense. BlackRock favors a multi-asset active approach amid uncertain conflict outcomes, avoiding big directional equity bets.

items.title: Upcoming Key Labor Market Data to Gauge Resilience Across Major Economies
items.summary: Labor market figures from the U.S., euro area, and Japan this week will test if recent softening signals broader cooling or continued strength. U.S. data follows weaker payrolls, with unemployment expected stable.

items.title: US New Home Sales Plunge 17.6% MoM, Missing Expectations
items.summary: U.S. New Home Sales dropped 17.6% month-over-month, far below the forecasted 3.9% gain, signaling housing market weakness. This comes amid broader economic data releases including ISM Services and Consumer Credit.

items.title: US Total Household Debt Hits $18.8 Trillion
items.summary: Total U.S. household debt reached $18.8 trillion, reflecting ongoing borrowing trends. Challenger Job Cuts rose to 60.62K, above expectations of 56K.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Rises 1.2% to $2.76T as Bitcoin Tops $81K Amid Iran Peace Talks
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market rebounded on Tuesday with Bitcoin climbing 3.5% to around $80,855 and the overall market cap reaching $2.76 trillion, driven by easing oil prices following hints of progress in U.S.-Iran peace negotiations. The rally triggered $225 million in short liquidations and moved the Crypto Fear and Greed index back to neutral territory.

items.title: CLARITY Act Finalized: Senate Reaches Stablecoin Yield Compromise
items.summary: U.S. Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks have signaled that a stablecoin yield compromise in the CLARITY Act is final, opposing banks' pushback on the provision. The legislation represents a significant step forward in crypto regulatory clarity.

items.title: SEC Delays Prediction Markets ETF Approvals for Polymarket and Kalshi
items.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has postponed the launch of prediction market ETFs tied to Kalshi, Polymarket, and other platforms, seeking further clarity before approval. The delay reflects ongoing regulatory scrutiny of prediction market products.

items.title: Ethereum Gains 1% While Altcoins Show Mixed Performance
items.summary: Ethereum traded above $2,381 with a 1% gain, while major altcoins including XRP, Solana, and Tron showed relatively muted price action. Top performers included Toncoin (TON), MemeCore (M), and Morpho (MORPHO).

items.title: Bitcoin Treasury Firm Strive Acquires $34M in BTC as Strategy Pauses Buying
items.summary: Bitcoin treasury firm Strive purchased $34 million worth of Bitcoin, though Michael Saylor's Strategy (MSTR) confirmed it did not acquire any Bitcoin this week, pausing its recent acquisition spree.

items.title: Telegram's Pavel Durov Reveals Major Updates on TON, Driving Price Surge
items.summary: Toncoin (TON) emerged as a top performer after Pavel Durov announced significant updates related to Telegram and the Toncoin ecosystem, contributing to the altcoin's strong daily performance.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Big Tech Cuts 80,000 Jobs in Q1 2026, Blames AI Disruption
items.summary: Major tech companies laid off over 80,000 employees in the first quarter of 2026, directly attributing the cuts to AI advancements, with firms now treating AI as employees rather than mere tools.

items.title: Bisq DEX Hit by AI-Assisted Hack, 11 BTC Stolen
items.summary: Decentralized exchange Bisq suffered a protocol exploit potentially carried out by an AI agent, resulting in the theft of 11 Bitcoin, raising alarms for self-custody security in the crypto ecosystem.

items.title: Hiring Manager Reveals 90% of AI Engineers Lack Core Skills
items.summary: A recruiter who hires AI engineers claims 90% are faking expertise, lacking fundamentals like bias-variance understanding, deep learning, and proper model training, relying instead on APIs.

items.title: AI Chip Demand Drives Tech Surge Ahead of AMD Earnings
items.summary: Tech stocks are rallying amid insatiable investor demand for AI chips, with AMD's upcoming earnings seen as a key indicator for the semiconductor sector beyond Nvidia.

items.title: SoundHound AI Stock Up 45% as AI Trading Picks Heat Up
items.summary: SoundHound AI (SOUN) has surged approximately 45% from recent lows, with traders watching for pullbacks amid broader AI stock momentum including BBAI and ARM.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Steam Controller Sells Out in 30 Minutes, Scalpers Prompt Gamer Backlash
items.summary: The relaunched Steam Controller sold out rapidly, leading to scalpers snapping up stock and gamers fighting back against resellers. Import records suggest a potential Steam Machine launch soon.
items.source: News Wave / Moore's Law Is Dead

items.title: New PS6 Details Emerge Online, Including Planned Features
items.summary: Leaked information online reveals upcoming PS6 features, sparking discussions on its potential influence on PC hardware alongside mentions of PS6 RAM specs.
items.source: News Wave / Moore's Law Is Dead

items.title: 007 First Light Announced with 20-Hour Campaign
items.summary: The upcoming 007 First Light game will feature a substantial 20-hour campaign, set for release later this month.

items.title: SEGA Announces LEGO Mega Drive Console
items.summary: SEGA has revealed a new LEGO-themed Mega Drive console, adding to nostalgic gaming hardware releases.

items.title: Warhorse Studios Teases New Lord of the Rings Game
items.summary: Developers of Kingdom Come: Deliverance, Warhorse Studios, have shared new details and teases for a major Lord of the Rings project in development.

items.title: Valve Import Records Hint at Steam Machine Arrival
items.summary: US import records for 'game consoles' from Valve suggest the Steam Machine or Steam Frame may be launching soon, following the Steam Controller release.

items.title: Clash of Clans Temporary Event Breaks Game with Golden Dragons
items.summary: For 24 hours, Clash of Clans gave every army 20 Golden Dragons, creating overpowered troops that demolished bases due to their massive HP and DPS.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Explores Chip Production with Intel and Samsung
items.summary: Apple held exploratory talks with Intel and visited Samsung's Texas plant to evaluate producing core processors for its devices in the US, addressing supply chain flexibility concerns. Intel's stock jumped over 12% on the news.

items.title: Elon Musk Settles SEC Dispute for $1.5 Million
items.summary: Musk resolved a years-long dispute with the SEC over his Twitter investment with a $1.5 million settlement without admitting wrongdoing.

items.title: iOS 26.5 Adds End-to-End Encryption for RCS Messages
items.summary: Apple's upcoming iOS 26.5 will introduce end-to-end encryption for RCS messages between Apple and Android devices, enhancing privacy for cross-platform communications.

items.title: Bose Launches New Lifestyle Home Audio Lineup
items.summary: Bose introduced a new Lifestyle home audio collection featuring a speaker, soundbar, and subwoofer, available starting May 15.

items.title: Form Releases Affordable Smart Swim 2 LT Goggles
items.summary: Form launched a more affordable version of its smart swimming goggles, the Smart Swim 2 LT, which removes heart-rate monitoring while retaining other features.
